Details: The oxidation of 17a-ethynyl estradiol (EE2) from water by chlorine oxidation can be effective and is dependent on the initial contaminant dose, chlorine dose, contact time and pH. One study presented the EE2 half life values of 73.2 minutes, 14.6 minutes and 7.3 minutes for chlorine doses of 0.1 mg/L, 0.5 mg/L and 1.0 mg/L. Another study showed that oxidation of EE2 by chlorine is rapid and near completion in the following conditions: pH of 5.5, 3.8 mg/L chlorine dose and 24 hour contact time.
